page: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, the instance features 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data highlights the instance's multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU extended instructions all ranking in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 6743.58 GB/sec and large block uncached read speeds at 337.58 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, falls within the average tier. The system also demonstrates strong performance in specialized tasks, including Geekbench ray tracing and image processing, which both rank in the top tier.

The c6i.metal instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps but does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage must be provisioned externally. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration eliminates hypervisor overhead, allowing direct access to the physical hardware. This makes it highly resource-dense for compute-heavy workloads, though the lack of bundled local storage requires careful architectural planning for data-intensive applications.

Given its high multi-core compute density and strong memory bandwidth, this instance is well-suited for high-performance computing, multi-threaded software compilation, intensive database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference. It is also highly appropriate for workloads requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz, 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory read bandwidth, alongside strong performance in software compilation and database operations, while single-core metrics remain average. This bare-metal instance offers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for compute-bound applications. It is best suited for high-performance computing, multi-threaded compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps networking, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform delivering dedicated multi-core compute power and high-speed networking for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
256 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier read bandwidth performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cryptographic instruction support
Bare-metal architecture eliminating hypervisor overhead for maximum resource density
Optimized for high-performance computing, database operations, and software compilation
